review_title: Marketo Magic!
description: We onboarded with Marketo recently and have been incredibly pleased with the seamless integration! Our sales and marketing team are using it with ease!
author: Mai M.
pros: Integrating Marketo to our team was a breeze and the customer support helped make the implementation seamless for everyone!
cons: Thus far, NO cons! The team has adapted quickly and we're already incorporating the platform into our daily routines.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A.Mullican Views on Marketo Engage Utilization
author: Andrew M.
pros: Marketo Engage facilitates robust marketing automation capabilities which help boost sales and in long run leads to high ROI.
cons: Ever since I incorporated Marketo Engage, I have never come across bugs when creating, customizing and sending outbound marketing emails.

review_title: Powerful workflows, but full-time help will be required.
description: Overall, my experience was poor...though there were a few highlights along the way. Again, the segmentation and workflow actioning that it provides are very powerful. But you're cowed in by a labyrinth of folders and redundant workflow rules that make it very hard to remain nimble once you've built the whole system out.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Powerful workflows, particularly when attached to lead nurturing email campaigns. If your team keeps a very clean Salesforce or CRM instance, there's an awful lot you can segment and action off of.
cons: If something is broken in the product, you'd better have a PhD in Marketo in order to track down exactly what it is. The product's usability and assorted tooltips leave a lot to be desired. This is why 95% of Marketo customers have to hire a full-time administrator to wield it. You either know how to use it from deep years of experience, or you're going to have to spend a lot of time figuring it out.

review_title: A Marketing Automation Platform that can do anything, any way.
description: Overall, Marketo was hands down my FAVORITE Marketing Automation Platform. the versatility and ability to do the same thing 100 ways is both a blessing and a curse; blessing if you create playbooks, curse if everyone does it their own way. Sit down with admins and users and determine major processes, workflows, and master lists together to save a headache later
author: Logan B.
pros: I love the way Marketo Engage's file structuring allows for really detailed nomenclature. in addition, the full customizability of leads and their data, fields, and querying off those fields is really great.
cons: I pretty strongly dislike the "Marketo Logic," where at times logic filters in Marketo do not make sense. It is important to take time to understand how Marketo handles IS, IS NOT, CONTAINS, and NOT CONTAINS to avoid sending mistakes.

review_title: Robust Application
description: I have a positive experience with Marketo Engage and would highly recommend it overall. The ability to analyze data captured by this application is amazing and we were able to capture great insights and pivot accordingly. The app is advanced and can become cumbersome and manual if managed incorrectly by a team, which is something we experienced at first.
author: Haley S.
pros: Marketo engage is extremely robust compared to other similar products I’ve used for lead management and marketing automation.
cons: I will admit it is not for the faint of heart! Teams using Marketo Engage will have the most success with experienced and technical Marketing Ops personnel to onboard the solution and engineer the respective workflows and automation according to their business.

review_title: Lead Management and Email Marketing Program for Sales Professionals
description: With Marketo Engage, I am able to send targeted content to audiences through lead nurturing tools which help boost sales leading to high ROI.
author: Martha B.
pros: Personalizing buyer journey, streamlining marketing strategies and sending outbound targeted marketing emails have been automated by Marketo Engage through workflow automation.
cons: Even though the user interface for Marketo Engage is dated back and less intuitive and never disappoints since it meets my sales and marketing needs.

review_title: Seamless Integration, Smarter Decisions: A Dive into Marketo
description: Adobe Marketo Engage stands out for its seamless integration with other Adobe tools, such as Adobe Target, providing a unified experience across digital touchpoints. Additionally, its advanced reporting capabilities help marketers evaluate campaign performance and make informed decisions based on data.
author: Yolandi E.
pros: Being familiar with the adobe layout I found it easy to use and very user friendly .
cons: I think if you are not familiar with the adobe layout one might struggle a little bit
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Easy and efficient
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I use Marketo to manage our email campaigns. I really like how organized it is. The system makes it easy to go back and pull data from previous campaigns and run custom reports. It makes it easier to collaborate across teams and I can find data myself without having to ask the campaign manager.
cons: The UI took some practice to feel comfortable navigating because it has so many options. Once I figured out what was needed for my role it became much easier.

review_title: Easy automation for campaigns that hit the bullseye.
description: It’s allowed us to scale our client visits much more efficiently thanks to automatically-followups and content personalization, which has resulted in higher engagement rates without having to stretch out our small team.
author: Imogen R. A.
pros: We were able to create far more intricate email campaigns with Marketo Engage letting us automate several steps we would otherwise have to conduct manually. I love the lead scoring and audience segmentation features, it help us make sure that we’re targeting the right people at the right time which is very important for a small marketing team like ours.
cons: It’s also not the most beginner-friendly platform. The process of establishing smart campaigns and integrations took longer than I had anticipated, and it’s almost necessary to have someone who has a technical background or extra training to make full use of it.

review_title: Long winded email distribution
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Keeps records and distribution lists up to date as you can easily make updates. Allows for easy approvals by other team members
cons: Hard to navigate, feels repetitive. convoluted ways of filtering distribution lists which leaves open to error.

review_title: Marketo Engage opinion of use
description: Marketo personalized my consumer experiences, marketing planning, and advertising, and had helpful information for improved decision-making for my online store.
author: Heather T.
pros: helps assist companies in understanding interactions with customers and growing marketing strategies.
cons: The steep learning curve of Marketo Engage is one of its drawbacks. For new users, the platform's variety of features and possibilities may be overwhelming. It takes a lot of time and work to fully understand and make use of its advanced features.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Solid lead generation tool to support sales teams
description: Generally a good experience overall and I would recommend this to others within our division
author: Steven B.
pros: Great way to do lead generation for our sales colleagues across subscriptions and sponsorship
cons: It does take some time to set up programmes and its important to agree with sales upfront what their expectations are
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Awesome solution for managing marketing campaigns all form one place
description: Marketo's made it so much easier to run, monitor, and report on our marketing efforts. We have it synced with our social channels, ad channels, and email and SMS platforms. It's main benefit has definitey been the time we've saved with it. Without it, we'd have to design and run our campaigns all by hand, whereas now, you can just whip up a campaign and run it across several platforms with one click. Other than that, it performs relatively well, has a lot of integrations, and isn't too overly priced. Would definitely recommend overall.
author: Janelle B.
pros: 1. We didn't have to set up a single API connection to integrate Marketo with our marketing platforms. We were able to connect our tools with it very very quickly thanks to the native integrations. 2. The campaign monitoring works great and has a lot of customization options. We can easily navigate between campaign types and we were able to add a lot of additional metrics onto the reports that weren't available out of the box.
cons: There's the odd issue with reports syncing late or not workng properly, but other than that, we haven't had anything that caused much trouble so far.

review_title: Powerfully Engage Your Customers with Marketo Engage: A Comprehensive Review
description: My overall experience with Marketo Engage Software has been positive. Marketo Engage has enabled us to create personalized customer journeys, allowing us to build relationships with our customers and increase engagement. We have been able to track our customer journey, understand their behavior and tailor our campaigns accordingly. This has resulted in improved customer retention and improved customer loyalty. Additionally, Marketo Engage has allowed us to automate processes, saving us time and money, and enabling us to focus more on customer-facing activities. Finally, Marketo Engage has given us a better understanding of our customers, allowing us to better target our campaigns and improve our ROI. All in all, Marketo Engage has been a great asset for our business.
author: Devendra Kumar S.
pros: 1. Easy-to-use interface: Marketo Engage has an intuitive user interface that makes it easy to quickly build complex marketing programs and campaigns.2. Advanced segmentation capabilities: Marketo Engage provides a powerful segmentation engine to target the right audiences and ensure maximum engagement.3. Automation and integration: Marketo Engage provides a wide range of automation capabilities, enabling users to save time and improve efficiency. It also allows for easy integration with other systems, such as Salesforce and Google Analytics.4. Flexible and customizable: Marketo Engage allows for easy customization of all marketing programs and campaigns, enabling users to tailor their activities to their specific needs.5. Comprehensive reporting: Marketo Engage provides comprehensive, real-time reporting on all activities, enabling users to track and measure their results.
cons: One of the cons of Marketo Engage software is that its user interface is not always intuitive. It can be difficult to navigate or find the features or settings you’re looking for. Additionally, the integration with existing business processes can be tricky and may require additional time and resources to ensure a successful integration. Additionally, the software can be quite expensive, which might be cost-prohibitive for some businesses. Finally, some features are missing from the software that could be useful, such as an AI-powered analytics engine or a more comprehensive CRM system.

review_title: I think it's a tool that has enough features to power marketing activities.
description: It's a deep tool, so it can be a bit difficult to use it perfectly, but with a lot of manual knowledge and an external community, we have an environment where you can easily leverage Marketo's performance. Also, for content such as emails and LPs that are used for marketing measures, once a template is created, all that's left is to combine each part like a jigsaw puzzle, so the speed of content creation has increased dramatically. not having coding skills like HTML can create high-quality content.
author: Ana A.
pros: Marketo Engage can intuitively set the focus for leads and customers, so it was easy to use even for me, who has little marketing experience. The integration with Salesforce is well developed, so both implementations will create synergies. In addition, it is possible to connect to other systems via Webhook, and this can also be sufficiently configured by reading the manual published by Adobe.
cons: When searching for content on Marketo, the search capability is low and it can be difficult to find the desired content, so it would be helpful if you could improve that as well.

review_title: Marketo's a fantastic tool to consolidate all our ads efforts
description: Marketo's truly a must-have if you have ad operations spread out over many channels. It brings together all your data to give you a fullstory view, it makes it much much easier to launch ads from one platform, and it produces great-looking charts to boost decisin-making. A great tool overall
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Marketo's campaign analytics features are great. It can handle large volumes of campaign data without stuttering, and can easily chart tables with a dozen custom metrics
cons: The interface is very fresh. It doesn't tak elong at all to get to grips with it. I was able to start using it confidently in less than two weeks

review_title: The best marketing solution-Marketo Engage
author: Winnie N.
pros: I I like the flexibility that comes with the tool which helps us to scale our marketing activities
cons: The tool works perfectly and we have not encountered any technicalities

review_title: Powerful marketing automation tool with a wide range of capabilities
author: Rares R.
pros: Definitely the wide range of features. From lead scoring and nurturing to email marketing, to hosting webinars, creating landing pages, and using forms, the platform has everything we need to execute complex campaigns and track our results. Integrations with a wide range of platforms and marketing tools are also a strong point of Marketo.
cons: I feel that there is a bit of a learning curve when it comes to using the platform. It's not the most intuitive system and it can take some time to get the hang of all the different tools and settings. Also, the user interface and some of the functionalities seem to be a bit old or outdated. The reporting section seems a bit complex and difficult to use. Difficult to keep the database healthy due to syncing errors with our CRM.

review_title: Best-in-class Marketing Automation Solution
author: Austin S.
pros: Marketo is the "Porsche with a manual transmission" of marketing automation platforms. It's full of cool features and is incredibly powerful. The strengths: 1) Workflow flexibility that is unmatched by other platforms. Building complex data management, lead scoring, and organizational processes (such as lead routing) is very achievable in Marketo. They basically give you every function they can possibly code and let their customers piece them together as they like (more on this in #7). 2) The engagement programs, primarily meant for lead nurturing, are truly best-in-class. It's a little bit of an adjustment at first, but it really simplifies complex nurturing tracks and saves marketers from putting prospects into multiple tracks at once (which happens more than most admit) 3) The vision is impressive. Marketo has a clear, long-term vision that they continually deliver on. Examples being advances in personalization, ABM, ect. I'd bet on them to lead the way (or buy the way via Vista ) to whatever is next in marketing automation, such as AI or machine learning.
cons: I love Marketo. But, there are some things I don't love. 1) The landing page builder has made improvements but still, leaves some to be desired. Unless a template available for import matches what you'd like to do, you pretty much have to hire a developer to build your templates. 2) The reporting is somewhat overhyped. They REALLY sell revenue cycle analytics, but in reality, it's tough to implement and even tougher to accurately maintain. Since data is essentially timestamped and not stored in an external record (i.e. like Salesforce has for a campaign member), if you mess it up, you're SOL. Marketo's advice to me was "don't mess up."

review_title: Marketo does a decent job with marketing campaigns; lackluster on the integrations and customization
description: Moderately positive; for smaller companies on a marketing budget who don't need more than email campaigns, this will work well for you. If you run an enterprise business who needs more, I suggest upgrading to Eloqua
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It was easy to setup and maintain our marketing campaigns; landing pages were customizable for the most part, and the tracking codes were easy to install and events triggered as promised. Cost is substantially less than premium marketing software, so that's a selling point.
cons: We migrated from Oracle/Eloqua, so we already had campaigns that we used. It was a total chore working with their teams to get everything carried over correctly (we never actually got every object, we had to re-create most). Most of the customization with batch jobs and custom fields didn't work as described in their documentation, and I got the feeling that they don't entertain a lot of big fish in the pond--maybe this has changed?

review_title: Marketo: An Ultimate Platform for marketing!
author: Praveen R.
pros: The reason why I referred the application as an ultimate platform is that it gathers all information about your digital marketing campaigns emails, online forms, marketing material etc. at a single place. It is a user-friendly interface which is easily understandable by you and it has a very brilliant capability of automating almost everything. In this, one can build a variety of things like email templates, landing pages, etc. It supports multi-touch campaigns and multichannel. It is broad and very useful for CRM Integration and most importantly Analytics. It increases efficiency in a variety of ways (like the ability to create a tokenized template) as well as its Lead management process is also brilliant!! Its capabilities are very vast, Comprehensive campaigns capabilities are one of them.
cons: Here are a few cons which shouldn’t be ignored. Reporting of Analytics of Marketo is very bad and in addiction smart list designs are not flexible and it is very difficult to design them. Most importantly it is not within the range of all types of people, as its rates are little bit higher. Its interface should be improved for new users as well as some software bugs should also be fixed regarding auto-saving. It loads the previewing list very slowly. Its Spam Identification feature also very poor as well as email and landing page analytics are also not enough. But at last, I want to say that the major issue with it, is regarding its speed of loading the application which needs a lot of improvement

review_title: All-in-one marketing and lead generation solution
author: Stephen K.
pros: I like that the tool provides a centralized and flexible solution to handle email marketing and lead generation
cons: The learning curve is complex especially to people new to marketing.

review_title: Pros and cons of Marketo Engage
author: Nzole M.
pros: i like that the tool is scalable and has a load set of comprehensive features which helps us deal with email marketing and customer engagement.
cons: The user interface is relatively not friendly and might be challenging for new users.

review_title: Adobe Marketo Engage is the world's leading marketing automation platform.
author: Francois Pascal N.
pros: This tool enables you to collect rich, up-to-date customer data, personalize your campaigns, automate your campaigns, share content and much more.
cons: It is not recommended for new businesses just starting out, as it is costly.

review_title: Powerful but steep learning curve
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Salesforce sync is more robust and configurable than hubspot. Ability to assign period costs to marketing campaigns
cons: The interface for a full featured product is very complicated. UI could be improved with quick wins streamlined ui
